data.title: Alfa Laval AB reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Alfa Laval AB (publ) press release (ALFVY): Q1 Earnings per share of SEK 4.59 (4.82).
  • Net sales decreased by -3 percent to SEK 15.9 (16.5) billion, with an organic increase of 2 percent.
  • Order intake was SEK 17.6 (17.8) billion, a decline of -1 percent. The organic increase was 6 percent.
  • Adjusted EBITA decreased by -1 percent to SEK 2.9 (2.9) billion, corresponding to a margin of 18.1 (17.7) percent.
  • Cash flow from operating activities amounted to SEK 1.2 (1.4) billion.

data.title: Hamilton Lane launches new interval fund, converts existing fund into interval fund
data.description:
Hamilton Lane (HLNE) launched the new Hamilton Lane Credit Income Fund and converted the Hamilton Lane Private Infrastructure Fund into an interval fund structure.

Hamilton Lane Credit Income Fund was declared effective last month, and has reached more than $350M in commitments.

Hamilton Lane Private Infrastructure Fund was initially launched in October 2024, and is set to continue its existing infrastructure strategy focused on co‑investments and secondaries.

The two interval funds are designed to offer institutional and private wealth investors access to private market strategies.

The features include 1099 tax reporting, quarterly limited liquidity, daily NAV pricing, and an investment minimum of $2,500 in certain share classes.

data.title: Tenax Therapeutics appoints Thomas Staab as chief financial officer
data.description:
• Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) has appointed Thomas Staab as chief financial officer, effective May 11, 2026, replacing Thomas McGauley.
  • Staab brings over 25 years of leadership experience across management and corporate finance roles in the healthcare industry, the company said. He most recently served as CFO and Secretary of LENSAR until May 2026 and Senior Vice President, CFO, and Treasurer at BioCryst Pharmaceuticals from July 2011 to February 2020.
  • TENX +0.88% premarket to $13.8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: P.F. Chang's eyes going public again in about two years
data.description:
P.F. Chang's appears to be more serious about planning for an IPO in the near future. CEO Jim Mazany said at the Restaurant Leadership Conference that the company is planning to go public in about two years, describing the move more as an internal goal rather than a set plan. He tied the public ambition to the chain's broader turnaround effort, saying the brand is trying to rebuild average unit volumes, lift dining-room sales, and expand both domestically and internationally before filing paperwork with the SEC for an IPO.

Notably, Mazany said he thinks the chain could triple its international store count to 300, according to the Restaurant Business website.

Looking back, P.F. Chang's was founded in 1993 in Scottsdale, Arizona, by Paul Fleming and Philip Chiang, combining Fleming’s restaurant experience with Chiang's family recipes and modern Chinese-American dishes. The chain grew quickly as it became known for signature items like lettuce wraps and wok-cooked dishes before going public in 1998 to set the stage for international expansion. In the 2010s, P.F. Chang’s changed hands through private-equity ownership, spun off its Pei Wei concept, and continued expanding abroad, including openings in China, Mexico City, and Kuwait City.

Other restaurant companies that analysts have circled as IPO candidates include Jersey Mike's, Panera Brands (PANERA), Fogo de Chão, Inspire Brands, 7 Brew, and Café Rio.

data.title: Tractor Supply is the most oversold consumer discretionary stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most oversold stocks in the consumer discretionary sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 consumer discretionary stocks ranked based on their lowest Relative Strength Index.

The list is topped by Tractor Supply Company (TSCO), with an RSI of 25. Lucid Group (LCID) and Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings (OLLI) are next, with McDonald’s (MCD) and Patrick Industries (PATK) rounding out the rest of the top five.

The list features a variety of industries within the consumer discretionary sector, including restaurants such as McDonald’s and Texas Roadhouse (TXRH), footwear with NIKE (NKE), and leisure facilities represented by Planet Fitness (PLNT). All top-ranked stocks on the list maintain an RSI below 50.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period—typically 14 days.

RSI readings of 70 or above are generally considered a signal that a stock may be overbought and potentially poised for a pullback. An RSI below 30 indicates that a stock may be oversold, signaling that intense selling pressure has potentially driven the price below its fair value.

Here is the list:

  1. Tractor Supply Company (TSCO), Market Cap: $20.81B, RSI: 25

  2. Lucid Group, Inc. (LCID), Market Cap: $2.58B, RSI: 32

  3. Ollie’s Bargain Outlet Holdings, Inc. (OLLI), Market Cap: $5.37B, RSI: 36

  4. McDonald’s Corporation (MCD), Market Cap: $214.56B, RSI: 39

  5. Patrick Industries, Inc. (PATK), Market Cap: $3.43B, RSI: 40

  6. CarMax, Inc. (KMX), Market Cap: $5.54B, RSI: 40

  7. NIKE, Inc. (NKE), Market Cap: $68.70B, RSI: 40

  8. Planet Fitness, Inc. (PLNT), Market Cap: $5.72B, RSI: 41

  9. Texas Roadhouse, Inc. (TXRH), Market Cap: $10.61B, RSI: 42

  10. LCI Industries (LCII), Market Cap: $2.94B, RSI: 44

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Philip Morris sees organic sales growth for both the smoke-free and combustibles businesses
data.description:
Philip Morris International (PM) fell slightly in early trading after the tobacco giant issued its Q1 earnings report. 

Revenue increased by 2.7% organically, mainly reflecting a favorable pricing variance driven by international combustibles. Those positive factors were partly offset by unfavorable volume/mix, mainly driven by lower international combustibles and U.S. volume. Both the smoke-free (up by 12.4%, 5.3% organically) and combustibles (up by 6.7%, 0.9% organically) businesses made meaningful contributions during the quarter.

Total shipment volume was down 1.9%, driven lower by a 5.1% decline in the cigarette business and a 16.1% drop in the oral SFP business.

Notably, PM's smoke-free business accounted for 43% of total net revenue in the quarter. The company's smoke-free products are now available in 108 markets.

Adjusted operating income increased by 0.9% on an organic basis. Non-GAAP EPS of $1.96 beat the consensus estimate of $1.83 and last year's mark of $1.69.

"Our performance exceeded our expectations in the first quarter, with an outstanding delivery from IQOS driving very good growth for the group against a strong prior-year comparison," highlighted CEO Jacek Olczak. "Building on excellent broad-based momentum in the international smoke-free business and 16% adjusted diluted EPS growth in Q1, we are well positioned to continue delivering best-in-class performance in 2026," he added.

Looking ahead, the tobacco giant sees full-year adjusted EPS of $8.36 to $8.51 (midpoint $8.435) vs. a prior expectation of $8.38 to $8.53 and the consensus estimate of $8.38. Full-year organic revenue growth of +5% to +7% is anticipated.

Shares of Philip Morris International (PM) fell 0.2% in premarket trading to $153.00. Altria (MO) and British American Tobacco (BTI) both showed minor gains in the early session.

data.title: SK hynix plans to invest nearly $13B in new South Korean facility amid AI memory demand
data.description:
SK hynix (HXSCL) is planning to ​invest 19T won (about $12.85B) ‌in a new manufacturing facility in South Korea for advanced packaging to ​meet the growing demand ​for AI memory, according to a regulatory filing.

On Wednesday, SK hynix carried out a groundbreaking ceremony for its P&T7 (Package & Test) facility, which will be built in Cheongju Technopolis Industrial Complex in Oebuk-dong, Heungdeok-gu, Cheongju-si, Chungcheongbuk-do, South Korea.

The South Korean tech giant said P&T7 is a dedicated advanced packaging fab essential for manufacturing AI memory products such as high-bandwidth ​memory, or HBM, ⁠chips.

"P&T7 is the core production base that will complete SK hynix’s AI memory leadership. We will focus our manufacturing capabilities to ensure that the advanced products manufactured here set the standard for global AI infrastructure," said Byeonggi Lee, head of manufacturing at SK hynix.

SK hynix and compatriot Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) are among the world's largest memory chipmakers. SK hynix is a major supplier of HBM chips to Nvidia (NVDA). Samsung and American company Micron Technology (MU) also compete with SK hynix in this space.

Update: The story was updated with SK hynix's regulatory filing.

data.title: Tenax Therapeutics names new CFO
data.description:
• Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) appointed Thomas R. Staab II as CFo, effective May 11, 2026, replacing interim CFO Thomas McGauley.
  • Staab brings more than 25 years of financial leadership experience in the healthcare sector. He most recently served as CFO and secretary at LENSAR.
  • Tenax (TENX) also announced inducement equity awards tied to the appointment, including 10,000 restricted stock units and options to purchase 450,000 common shares
